DECLARATION OF SUZANNE KLOTZ IN OPPOS. TO DEFT'S MTN FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION INCLUDING OBJECTION TO STEELEY DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF WINEBERG, WILLIAM A. AN INDIVIDUAL

(******CONTINUATION OF RULING ON DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND JOSHUA JENNINGS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION******) PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM UNDER THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION REGARDING THE CONSIDERATION OF EVIDENCE OUTSIDE OF THE RECORD IS ALSO MOOT AND THE DEFENDANT PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT THERE IS NO REASON TO BELIEVE THAT THE HEARING OFFICER WILL MAKE THAT MISTAKE AGAIN AND THAT THE CITY HAS MADE SUFFICIENT CHANGES TO THE ORDINANCE CODIFYING THE REQUIREMENT THAT EVIDENCE OUTSIDE THE RECORD SHALL NOT BE CONSIDERED. THE BURDEN THUS SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT THE HEARING OFFICER CONTINUES TO VIOLATE THAT REQUIREMENT AND HAS FAILED TO DO SO. ACCORDINGLY, THERE IS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING THIS ISSUE. BASED ON THE ABOVE, PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO DECLARATORY OR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND JOSHUA JENNINGS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION WAS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. ----------- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. AS TO THE 1ST & 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION (VIOLATION OF DUE PROCESS UNDER THE CALIFORNIA AND UNITED STATES CONSTITUTIONS), THE COURT FINDS THAT THE ORDINANCE IS NOT CONSTITUTIONALLY VAGUE. SEE FORD DEALERS ASSN. V. DEPT. OF MOTOR VEHICLES, (1982) 32 CAL.3D 347; HABITAT TRUST FOR WILDLIFE V. CITY OF RANCHO CUCAMONGA, (1009) 15 CAL.APP.4TH 1306. MOREOVER, "WHILE AN ENACTMENT MAY BE REDUNDANT, IT IS NOT VAGUE." ACLU V. BD. OF EDUCATION, (1963) 59 CAL.2D 203. PLAINTIFF'S FACIAL CHALLENGE TO THE PREDEPRIVATION REVIEW ALSO FAILS. LOVE V. MONTEREY, (1995) 37 CAL.APP.4TH 580; PEOPLE V. AMOR, (1974) 12 CAL.3D 20, ROTH V. LOS ANGELES, (1975) 53 CAL.APP.3D 679. AS LONG AS A PROMPT PREDEPRIVATION REVIEW IS PROVIDED BY THE AGENCY ADMINISTERING THE STATUTE, THE STATUTE IS VALID EVEN IF THE STATUTE DOES NOT EXPRESSLY PROVIDE FOR IT. MOREOVER, THE RECORD CLEARLY SHOWS EVIDENCE THAT THE REVIEW IS ADEQUATE UNDER MACKEY V. MONTRYUM (1979) 443 U.S. 1. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO MEET THE BURDEN OF SHOWING A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT IT IS NOT. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS UNDER THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION CONCERNING THE NOTICE AND THE PENALTIES ASSESSED AGAINST HIM ARE MOOT. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENTS THAT THE NOTICE FAILS TO COMPLY WITH SECTION 3715 WAS NEVER PLED IN THE COMPLAINT OR AMENDED COMPLAINT AND CANNOT BE RAISED AT THIS TIME. (******SEE NEXT ENTRY FOR CONTINUATION OF RULING******)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF WILLIAM WINEBERG'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION/ FOR RELIEF FROM ORDER. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO AMEND ITS ORIGINAL ORDER, SPECIFICALLY THE PORTION WHERE THE COURT STRIKING THE PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION. HOWEVER, THE COURT DID CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IN REACHING ITS ORIGINAL DECISION AND, THEREFORE, FINDS NO BASIS TO RECONSIDER ITS ORIGINAL DECISION OR CHANGE IT OTHER THAN INDICATED ABOVE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF WILLIAM WINEBERG'S MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF FEES UNDER EITHER SECTION 1021.5 OR 1988(B). PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN ANY OF THE FOUR ELEMENTS FOR AN AWARD OF FEES UNDER SECTION 1021.5. PLAINTIFF CANNOT RECOVER FEES UNDER SECTION 1988 BECAUSE HE DID NOT OBTAIN A JUDGMENT ON THE MERITS OR A COURT DECREE THAT MATERIALLY ALTERED THE RELATIONSHIP OF THE PARTIES. ORDER SIGNED. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
Off Calendar Mar-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tara M. Steeley, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4655, appearing for Defendant and moving party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND JOSHUA JENNINGS' Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs - The motion to tax is granted in its entirety. Assuming that the costs memorandum is timely, plaintiff is not the prevailing party within the meaning of section 1032(a)(4). Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM A. WINEBERG, TEL. NO. 434-1200, APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. TARA M. STEELEY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4655 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF WILLIAM WINEBERG'S Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees is Denied. Plaintiff is not entitled to an award of fees under either section 1021.5 or 1988(b). Plaintiff has not shown any of the four elements for an award of fees under section 1021.5. Plaintiff cannot recover fees under section 1988 because he did not obtain a judgment on the merits or a court decree that materially altered the relationship of the parties. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-24-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND JOSHUA JENNINGS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: George Benetatos, Esq. 398-2296 for pltf & opposing party; Tara M. Steeley, Deputy City Atty 554-4655 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted as modified -
Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted.
As to the 1st and 2nd causes of action (violation of due process under the California and United States Constitutions), the Court finds that the Ordinance is not Constitutionally vague. See Ford Dealers Assn. v. Dept. of Motor Vehicles, (1982) 32 Cal.3d 347; Habitat Trust for Wildlife v. City of Rancho Cucamonga, (1009) 15 Cal.app.4th 1306. Moreover, "while an enactment may be redundant, it is not vague." ACLU v. Bd. Of Education, (1963) 59 Cal.2d 203.
Plaintiff's facial challenge to the predeprivation review also fails. Love v. Monterey, (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 580; People v. Amor, (1974) 12 Cal.3d 20, Roth v. Los Angeles, (1975) 53 Cal.App.3d 679. As long as a prompt predeprivation review is provided by the agency administering the statute, the statute is valid even if the statute does not expressly provide for it. Moreover, the record clearly shows evidence that the review is adequate under Mackey v. Montryum (1979) 443 U.S. 1. Plaintiff fails to meet the burden of showing a triable issue of fact that it is not.
Plaintiff's claims under the 3rd cause of action concerning the notice and the penalties assessed against him are moot. Plaintiff's arguments that the notice fails to comply with Section 3715 was never pled in the complaint or amended complaint and cannot be raised at this time.
Plaintiff's claim under the 5Th cause of action regarding the consideration of evidence outside of the record is also moot and the defendant presented sufficient evidence to show that there is no reason to believe that the hearing officer will make that mistake again and that the City has made sufficient changes to the ordinance codifying the requirement that evidence outside the record shall not be considered. The burden thus shifted to plaintiff to present evidence that the hearing officer continues to violate that requirement and has failed to do so. Accordingly, there is no triable issue of material fact regarding this issue.
Based on the above, Plaintiff is not entitled to declaratory or injunctive relief.
Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
